questionText: Complete the sentence: 'The president ______ to the new law by the population last week.'
explanation: The action happened 'last week' (past time marker), and 'the president' is singular, so the passive form in past simple is 'was opposed'.

questionText: Which sentence is correct in the passive voice?
options: The homework is did by the student.
The homework was done by the student.
The homework were done by the student.
The homework has done by the student.
explanation: Passive voice: be + past participle. 'Homework' is singular, past simple 'was done' is correct. 'Did' is active past, not passive.

questionText: Choose the best passive form: 'The roads ______ (repair) at the moment.'
explanation: 'At the moment' indicates present continuous passive. 'Roads' plural → 'are being repaired'.

questionText: Transform to passive: 'Someone has stolen my phone.'
explanation: Present perfect active → present perfect passive: 'has been + past participle'.

questionText: Why is the passive voice used in: 'The suspect was arrested last night.' ?
options: Because the agent is unknown or unimportant
explanation: The passive is used when the agent (who arrested) is unknown or irrelevant. The focus is on the suspect.

options: The letter was written by Marie.
explanation: 'They have finished the project' is active (subject 'they' performs the action). The others have 'be + past participle'.

questionText: Choose the correct passive: 'The government will announce the new measures tomorrow.'
options: The new measures will be announced tomorrow.
The new measures are announced tomorrow.
The new measures were announced tomorrow.
The new measures have been announced tomorrow.
explanation: Future simple active → future simple passive: 'will be + past participle'.

questionText: Which sentence emphasizes the action, not the doer?
options: The chef prepared a delicious meal.
explanation: Passive voice focuses on the action/object ('a delicious meal'), not who did it.

questionText: Correct this passive sentence: 'The children was took to the museum.'
options: The children were taken to the museum.
The children was taken to the museum.
The children were took to the museum.
The children is taken to the museum.
explanation: 'Children' is plural → 'were'. Past participle of 'take' is 'taken', not 'took'.

questionText: Identify the correct passive form: 'The bridge ______ (build) for two years now.'
explanation: 'For two years now' suggests present perfect. Present perfect passive = 'has been + past participle'. (Note: 'has been being built' is rare and unnatural.)

questionText: Choose the best option for: 'Nobody has cleaned the classroom.' (Passive)
explanation: Present perfect negative active → present perfect negative passive: 'hasn't been cleaned'.

questionText: In which situation is the passive MOST appropriate?
options: When the agent is very important
When the result is more important than the doer
When the subject is a famous person
explanation: Passive focuses on the action/result, not who performed it (e.g., 'The window was broken' – result matters).

questionText: Transform to passive: 'They were painting the house when I arrived.'
options: The house was painted when I arrived.
The house was being painted when I arrived.
The house is being painted when I arrived.
The house had been painted when I arrived.
explanation: Past continuous active → past continuous passive: 'was/were + being + past participle'.

questionText: Which sentence uses the passive incorrectly?
options: The package is deliver by the postman.
explanation: 'is deliver' is wrong; should be 'is delivered' (past participle).

questionText: Choose the best passive: 'People speak English all over the world.'
options: English is spoken all over the world.
English was spoken all over the world.
English has been spoken all over the world.
English is being spoken all over the world.
explanation: Present simple active → present simple passive: 'is spoken' (general truth).
